gene_description: Apolipoprotein A-V — key regulator of plasma triglyceride levels. Modulates lipoprotein lipase activity and VLDL production. Variants significantly elevate triglycerides and metabolic syndrome risk.

source.title: Apolipoprotein A5 fifteen years anniversary: Lessons from genetic epidemiology
source.finding: APOA5 variants rs3135506 (S19W) and rs662799 (-1131T>C) are consistently associated with 15-16% higher triglyceride levels per minor allele and ~1.5-fold increased metabolic syndrome risk. Significant gene-diet interactions with dietary fat intake.

GG.effect: Homozygous S19W carriers have impaired APOA5 signal peptide processing, reducing protein secretion. Triglycerides elevated ~30% above baseline. Increased risk of metabolic syndrome, hypertriglyceridemia, and cardiovascular disease. Atherogenic LDL levels also elevated.
GG.recommendation: Strict dietary fat management — limit saturated fat, avoid trans fats. Omega-3 supplementation (2-4g EPA+DHA daily). Consider fibrate therapy if triglycerides remain >500 mg/dL. Reduce refined carbohydrates and alcohol. Regular triglyceride monitoring.

CG.effect: Heterozygous carriers show ~15% higher triglycerides. Modest increase in metabolic syndrome risk. Postprandial triglyceride clearance is somewhat delayed.
CG.recommendation: Heart-healthy diet with moderate fat intake. Regular triglyceride testing. Omega-3 rich diet (fatty fish 2-3x/week). Limit alcohol and refined sugars.

GC.effect: Heterozygous carriers show ~15% higher triglycerides. Modest increase in metabolic syndrome risk. Postprandial triglyceride clearance is somewhat delayed.
GC.recommendation: Heart-healthy diet with moderate fat intake. Regular triglyceride testing. Omega-3 rich diet (fatty fish 2-3x/week). Limit alcohol and refined sugars.

CC.effect: Wild-type with efficient APOA5 secretion and triglyceride regulation from this locus.

Homozygous -1131C carriers have ~50% lower APOA5 gene expression. Substantially elevated triglycerides (~32% above baseline). High metabolic syndrome risk (OR ~1.5). After Mediterranean diet intervention, these carriers show greater triglyceride reduction, indicating strong gene-diet interaction.
CC.recommendation: Mediterranean dietary pattern strongly recommended — shows greatest benefit in this genotype. Strict triglyceride management. Omega-3 supplementation. Limit refined carbohydrates and alcohol.

TC.effect: Heterozygous carriers show ~16% higher triglycerides. Intermediate APOA5 expression. Moderate metabolic syndrome risk elevation.
TC.recommendation: Mediterranean-style diet preferred. Regular lipid monitoring. Moderate omega-3 intake.

CT.effect: Heterozygous carriers show ~16% higher triglycerides. Intermediate APOA5 expression. Moderate metabolic syndrome risk elevation.
CT.recommendation: Mediterranean-style diet preferred. Regular lipid monitoring. Moderate omega-3 intake.

TT.effect: Wild-type with full APOA5 promoter activity and normal triglyceride regulation.
